title: params=None escape hatch in build_shielded_tx is a production footgun
description: build_shielded_tx accepts params: Option<&(SpendParameters, OutputParameters)> with None meaning 'skip proof generation'. The doc says 'Production callers must always pass Some'. A new caller who copies the function signature and passes None will get ParamsNotLoaded at runtime with no compile-time guard. The test helper call_build_for_error passes None explicitly, which is the only legitimate use. Scenario that triggers the cost: a contributor adds a payment code path, copies the existing call site, accidentally passes None or wires it wrong, and gets a runtime error instead of a compile-time error. Maintenance consequence: every future call site must be audited for the None case; there is no type-system enforcement. An alternative that would eliminate this: a newtype ParamsRef that cannot be None, with test helpers using a separate test-only builder function that stops before the proof step.
close_reason: Deliberate design: None is documented in send_payment doc comment as test-only; production callers must pass Some(params). The option is needed because SpendParameters has no Default and tests that exit before the proof step cannot construct a dummy.
